description: Lock Lens Grinding Workshops to spectacle-lens grinding and mounting craft, with early glass/crystal lenses as lineage and without making the broader optical_workshops node a prerequisite.

checks.reason: The broad medieval date remains tied to thirteenth-century northern Italian spectacle-lens craft while later shop documentation is captured in the uncertainty note.
Spectacle demand and manufacture supplied the direct product context for specialized lens-grinding and mounting shops.
Earlier glass and crystal lens craft is the material and optical lineage for later spectacle-lens grinding.
Guild and shop structures could host or regulate spectacle makers without being a universal dedicated spectacle-guild requirement.
Optical Workshops is the broader workshop node and must not be used as a direct prerequisite for its lens-grinding specialization.
